@@ -245,7 +245,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-gc4hz","title":"retry: cancelled operations must not count as circuit-breaker failures","description":"with_retry_and_circuit_cx pattern-matches Err(_) and calls circuit.record_failure() unconditionally. Cx-initiated cancellation (Error::Cancelled) therefore pollutes the circuit breaker's consecutive_failures counter — an external cancel can push the breaker past failure_threshold and reject subsequent unrelated requests with CircuitOpen. Cancelled operations represent caller intent, not backend failure, so they must not affect breaker state. Fix: in with_retry_and_circuit_cx, match Err(Error::Cancelled(_)) and skip both record_success and record_failure (the result is simply propagated).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:53:13.437155Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:22:33.645671Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:22:33.644374Z","close_reason":"Landed in 503c9506 (retry: exempt Error::Cancelled from circuit-breaker failure count) + 765743d5 (regression test) + 79029eeb (test fix: private fields -> snapshot()).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-gfawa","title":"frankenterm-core lib build blocked by web_framework asupersync type skew","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:10:44.813713Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T05:42:11.511779Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T05:42:11.511187Z","close_reason":"Fixed in efbf601c","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-gjjew","title":"retry loop retries operation-level cancellation instead of surfacing it","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:31:49.054890Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:38:18.464865Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:38:18.463876Z","close_reason":"Verified in 464ad9b4; further cargo proof blocked by ft-h6zod ingest.rs:2926 type inference regression","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-gk2ng","title":"runtime_async doctrine docs still treat runtime_compat as current API","description":"Reality-check finding after ft-y378j.4 removed the runtime_compat alias and scripts/check_runtime_compat_residuals.sh now scopes old-name references out of code. Several operator-facing doctrine/baseline docs still present runtime_compat as the active/current async API surface instead of historical context: docs/asupersync-migration-baseline.md:49-97 lists runtime_compat:: counts, highest-concentration file crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_compat.rs, and canonical target mappings through runtime_compat; docs/adr/0012-asupersync-runtime-doctrine.md:36-51 says prefer runtime_compat/cx boundaries and maps tokio::time::* to runtime_compat::*; docs/asupersync-architecture-doctrine.md has the same active guidance. README.md:41 now says the deprecated alias has been removed and runtime_async is canonical, so these docs can steer agents back toward a removed API name even though CI only guards Rust source. Fix: refresh the doctrine/baseline docs to say runtime_async for current guidance, move runtime_compat counts into dated historical sections, and ensure validation docs distinguish historical migration evidence from current API names.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T23:50:02.856218Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T00:04:51.883520Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-gk2ng","title":"runtime_async doctrine docs still treat runtime_compat as current API","description":"Reality-check finding after ft-y378j.4 removed the runtime_compat alias and scripts/check_runtime_compat_residuals.sh now scopes old-name references out of code. Several operator-facing doctrine/baseline docs still present runtime_compat as the active/current async API surface instead of historical context: docs/asupersync-migration-baseline.md:49-97 lists runtime_compat:: counts, highest-concentration file crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_compat.rs, and canonical target mappings through runtime_compat; docs/adr/0012-asupersync-runtime-doctrine.md:36-51 says prefer runtime_compat/cx boundaries and maps tokio::time::* to runtime_compat::*; docs/asupersync-architecture-doctrine.md has the same active guidance. README.md:41 now says the deprecated alias has been removed and runtime_async is canonical, so these docs can steer agents back toward a removed API name even though CI only guards Rust source. Fix: refresh the doctrine/baseline docs to say runtime_async for current guidance, move runtime_compat counts into dated historical sections, and ensure validation docs distinguish historical migration evidence from current API names.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T23:50:02.856218Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T00:07:56.801645Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T00:07:56.801399Z","close_reason":"Refreshed runtime doctrine docs to make runtime_async current and runtime_compat historical.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-gkqej","title":"[STRATEGIC/LOW] Audit-fatigue markers in project memory ('don't re-sweep') age poorly under high-concurrency agent commits — regression blindspot","description":"Modes-of-reasoning F6: MEMORY.md contains 'patterns-subsystem-bug-sweep-2026-04-20 [don't re-sweep]', similar for capture-storage, mux-term-codec. Combined with (a) 200+ recent commits, (b) 50+ agent identities committing concurrently, these 'clean' verdicts age fast. Regressions into already-audited modules escape scrutiny. Fix: add a git-SHA anchor to audit-cleared flags — auto-invalidate when the subsystem has >N commits since the audit. Or remove the 'don't re-sweep' advisories.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-24T19:16:26.931805Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T03:50:21.504131Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T03:50:21.503845Z","close_reason":"All 4 children landed: .1 ft-hph8i (frontmatter backfill), .2 ft-nedq3 (audit_marker_doctor f34eb572), .3 ft-cqhfe (skill template SHA frontmatter), .4 ft-l01pf (MEMORY_FORMAT.md ca30c3de). Audit-fatigue marker staleness pipeline now operational.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","modes-of-reasoning","process"]}
 {"id":"ft-gofz5","title":"[EPIC] Release engineering: first tagged release, cargo install validation, and binary distribution pipeline","description":"# Goal
Establish a working release pipeline so that \`cargo install --git ... ft\` actually works, release binaries are available for major platforms, and there are tagged versions users can reference.

# Background
The reality check found:
- Only 1 git tag exists ("backup-before-rewrite") — no version releases
- \`cargo install\` is advertised in README Quick Install but never validated
- 6 GitHub Actions workflows exist (ci.yml, release.yml, etc.) but release.yml has never been triggered for a real release
- The project is pre-1.0 with 4,288 commits but zero releases
- Build is resource-intensive (~793k LOC core library) which may cause cargo install to fail on memory-constrained machines

# Why This Matters
A project that can't be installed by its advertised method has zero users. Even for internal/swarm use, tagged releases let agents pin to known-good versions. Release hygiene signals project maturity.

# Architectural Intent
1. Validate that \`cargo install --git ... ft\` succeeds on a clean machine (Linux x86_64, macOS arm64 at minimum).
2. Create v0.1.0 tag with honest release notes reflecting current capabilities.
3. Ensure release.yml workflow produces downloadable binaries.
4. Add a CI job that runs \`cargo install\` from git to catch install regressions.

# Acceptance Criteria
1. \`cargo install --git https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm.git ft\` succeeds on Linux x86_64 and macOS arm64.
2. A v0.1.0 tag exists with release notes that honestly describe working features, partial features, and known limitations.
3. release.yml produces downloadable binaries for at least Linux x86_64 and macOS arm64.
4. CI includes a cargo install smoke test that runs on every push to main.
5. README Quick Install section includes minimum system requirements (RAM, disk, Rust nightly version).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:55:35.783442Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:07:18.344985Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:07:18.344593Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","distribution","goal-line","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-gofz5","depends_on_id":"ft-d0ez0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:59:00.127254Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-gofz5.1","title":"Validate cargo install from git on clean Linux x86_64 and macOS arm64 environments","description":"# Goal
Prove that the advertised Quick Install command actually works on fresh machines.

# Design
1. On a clean Linux x86_64 environment (CI runner or fresh container):
   - Install Rust nightly (matching rust-toolchain.toml)
   - Run: cargo install --git https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm.git ft
   - Verify: ft --version produces output
   - Verify: ft doctor runs without crash
   - Record: peak RAM usage, disk usage, wall time

2. On macOS arm64 (CI runner or local):
   - Same steps as above
   - Additional: verify LLVM/clang dependency resolution (cc alias issue documented in MEMORY.md)

3. If install fails, diagnose and fix:
   - Likely issues: memory exhaustion (793k LOC), missing system deps, cc alias conflict
   - Fix in Cargo.toml, build.rs, or CI configuration
   - Document minimum requirements

# Acceptance Criteria
1. cargo install succeeds on both platforms with exit code 0.
2. ft --version and ft doctor both work post-install.
3. Peak RAM and disk requirements documented.
4. If build requires CC/CXX overrides, document in README.","notes":"2026-04-07 CyanIvy: install/docs command corrected; targeted docs smoke test passes via rch; clean-host harness now passes and records WezTerm bridge prerequisites honestly; remaining gap is true clean-host cargo-install-from-git validation on Linux/macOS because rch in this environment is classifying cargo install as non-offloaded. Added workflow .github/workflows/cargo-install-validation.yml for that next step.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:55:46.599217Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T01:59:46.274240Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T01:59:46.261176Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","install","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-gofz5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-gofz5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:55:46.599217Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
